back: It is the process of predicting and managing IT resource requirements to meet current and future business demands while optimizing costs and performance.

front: What is Demand Forecasting in capacity planning?
back: The process of predicting future IT resource needs based on historical data, business growth projections, and seasonal patterns.
A reference point that establishes normal system performance metrics under typical workload conditions, used for comparison during capacity analysis.
When IT resources exceed actual requirements, leading to unnecessary capital expenditure and wasted infrastructure.
When allocated IT resources are insufficient to meet demand, causing service degradation, slow response times, and potential system failures.

front: What is a Service Level Agreement (SLA)?
back: A contract defining expected service performance metrics, including availability, response time, and throughput guarantees between IT provider and business.
The process of examining application and system workloads to understand resource consumption patterns and identify optimization opportunities.
Adding more resources (CPU, RAM, storage) to an existing server or infrastructure component to increase its capacity.
Adding more servers or nodes to distribute workload across multiple machines, improving scalability and fault tolerance.
Analyzing historical performance data over time to identify patterns and predict future resource needs and usage trends.
The percentage of available IT resources (CPU, memory, storage, bandwidth) that are actively being used at any given time.
The practice of ensuring IT strategies, investments, and capabilities directly support and enable business objectives and requirements.
A decline in system performance, reliability, or functionality below acceptable levels, often caused by resource constraints or overload.
Creating mathematical or simulation models to predict how systems will perform under various load conditions and resource configurations.

front: What is the primary goal of IT Capacity Planning?
back: To ensure right-sized IT resources are available at the right time, right cost, and right quality to support business operations and growth.
